metadata.title: Smart Route Planning on the Atsign Platform
metadata.description: Hybrid migration of Intel's Smart Route Planning Agent with a zero-trust policy plane. LangGraph reasoning reused as a headless backend; transport swapped for encrypted, port-less identity-to-identity messaging. Data sources publish from their own Atsign. Flutter commuter app + Gradio operator console. The planner asks a Policy Manager whether an Atsign may publish/request/view.
metadata.author: AI Architect MCP

data.label: Roadside Cameras & Sensors
data.notes:
Physical roadside cameras/sensors. Each intersection agent analyses its **own** local feed — shown here as one representative source.

- **Reuse:** maps to the Intel Scene Intelligence input (camera + vision pipeline).
- **Demo mode:** no real cameras needed — replaced by replay of the bundled `data/csv/*.csv`.
- Provides raw data: vehicle density, weather, incidents.

data.label: Intersection Agent — Market St
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@intxn_market_st`

Edge AI agent at the Market St intersection. Computer-vision scene analysis on its local camera (Intel Scene Intelligence).

- **ADD (new — not in the repo):** a publisher reusing `schema.py` `LiveTrafficData`.
- **Demo:** replays bundled CSV; swap in real Scene Intelligence later — same data shape.
- Publishes `live_traffic.smartroute`: name, coords, density, weather, incident.
- Own Atsign; **encrypted** push; never opens an inbound port.

data.label: Intersection Agent — 5th Ave
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@intxn_5th_ave`

Second distributed intersection agent — identical role, different location.

- **ADD (new):** same publisher build, reusing `schema.py` `LiveTrafficData`.
- **Extensibility:** a new intersection joins by registering a new Atsign in the `.smartroute` namespace — **policy-gated**, no code change on the planner.
- Analyses its own camera; pushes encrypted live traffic; no open inbound ports.

data.label: Intersection Agent — Broadway
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@intxn_broadway`

Third distributed intersection agent.

- **ADD (new):** same publisher pattern, reusing `schema.py`.
- Demonstrates **fan-in**: the planner aggregates updates from many independent, geographically separate agents knowing only their Atsigns — no network address.
- Analyses its local feed for congestion/weather/incidents.

data.label: Weather Feed
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@weather_feed`

Weather-conditions provider for points along candidate routes.

- **Reuse:** wraps the existing `weather_report.py` controller (`RouteStatusInterface`); publishes `schema.py` `WeatherData` (condition, temperature, visibility).
- **Demo:** replays `data/csv/weather_report.csv`; point at a real weather provider later — no planner change.
- Publishes `weather.smartroute`; own Atsign; encrypted push, no inbound port.

data.label: Traffic Trends Feed
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@traffic_trends_feed`

Historical traffic-trends provider for route points.

- **Reuse:** wraps `traffic_trends.py`; publishes `TrafficTrendsData` (vehicle_count, avg_speed, congestion_level).
- **Demo:** replays `data/csv/traffic_trends.csv`.
- Publishes `traffic_trends.smartroute`; own Atsign; encrypted push.

data.label: Planned Events Feed
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@events_feed`

Planned-events provider (concerts, games, closures) affecting routes.

- **Reuse:** wraps `planned_events.py`; publishes `PlannedEventsData` (event_name, congestion_level).
- **Demo:** replays `data/csv/planned_events.csv`.
- Publishes `planned_events.smartroute`; own Atsign; encrypted push.

data.label: Map Tile Service
data.notes:
External third-party map/tile provider for rendering the route map (OpenStreetMap tiles via Folium/Leaflet on the operator console; a mobile map widget on the commuter app).

- **KEEP:** unchanged from the Intel code.
- Lives **outside** the app; reached over its public HTTP interface.
- The **only** outward web dependency. No Atsign (not part of the network).

data.label: Smart Route Planner
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@smartroute_planner`

Headless backend agent — LangGraph reasoning + the comms layer. No UI of its own.

- **KEEP (unchanged):** `route_planner.py` (LangGraph), `planner_state.py`, `route_service.py`, `utils/*`, `schema.py`. (`main.py`'s UI now runs as the Operator Console.)
- **SWAP:** body of `live_traffic.py` — reads a cache filled by subscriptions, not polling. `RouteStatusInterface` unchanged, so the graph is untouched.
- **ADD:** a subscriber for all `.smartroute` publishers; **pushes** route + reroute alerts to commuter apps and the operator console.
- **Trust:** consults the Policy Manager (not a hardcoded list) to authorize publishers/clients.
- Own Atsign; encrypted; **no inbound port**.

data.label: Route & Map Data
data.notes:
**KEEP (unchanged):** the bundled GPX routes (`data/routes/*.gpx`).

- Candidate routes the planner picks from to compute the shortest direct route and alternates.
- Held privately by `@smartroute_planner`; read via `gpx_parser` / `route_service`.
- (The weather/traffic/events CSVs now sit behind their own feed Atsigns.)

data.label: Commuter Mobile App (Flutter)
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@commuter01`

**ADD (new):** Flutter mobile app — the commuter's surface. Holds the commuter's own Atsign.

- Sends start/destination to the planner and **receives pushed route updates + reroute alerts** in real time (even when backgrounded).
- Renders the live route on a map widget; pops an alert when the route changes (flood, accident, congestion).
- Reuses the planner's route geometry (`RoutePoints` in `schema.py`); no open ports; works over cellular/NAT.

data.label: Commuter
data.notes:
A driver/traveller. Uses the mobile app to ask for the best route and gets live reroute alerts on the move.

- Identified by their own Atsign (held by the app); reaches the planner privately — no account or password.
- In production each commuter has their own Atsign and app install.

data.label: Operator Console (Gradio)
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@route_operator`

**KEEP (reused):** Intel's Gradio UI (`main.py`), reframed as the city control-room console.

- Monitors **all** routes, intersections, and the agent's reasoning/status across the network.
- Receives live updates pushed by the planner; renders the Folium map (`map_creator.py`).
- Own Atsign; the operator proves identity to view — no inbound port.

data.label: Operator
data.notes:
Control-room / traffic-authority staff. Watches the whole network — every intersection, all active routes, and why the agent rerouted.

- Identified by their own registered Atsign; only authorised operators may view.

data.label: Policy Manager
data.notes:
**Suggested Atsign:** `@route_policy`

**ADD (new):** the trust plane — a centralized, zero-trust **policy engine** with its own Atsign.

- The planner does **not** hardcode who may join. It asks the engine: *is this Atsign allowed to publish live data, request routes, or view the network?* — **default-deny**.
- **Policies are stored as encrypted records in the engine's own Atsign store** by default; pluggable to an external **database** for scale.
- Evaluates rules by **identity + role + action**; the **Policy Admin** maintains them.
- New publishers join **only when policy permits** → safe dynamic onboarding; segregation of duties (security owns policy, not the app code).

data.label: Policy Admin
data.notes:
Security / operations admin who owns the access rules. Separate from the city Operator (segregation of duties).

- Defines which Atsigns may publish, request, or view — by identity and role.
- Identified by their own registered Atsign.
